audit_items.proof_trace: do_item_get (line 3265) increments the item refcount; every other early-return path (CAS mismatch L3276, non-numeric L3283) calls do_item_remove(it) before returning, but the 'nbytes <= 2' guard at L3271–3273 returns NON_NUMERIC without calling do_item_remove(it), permanently leaking one refcount unit per call. The leaked refcount prevents item_free from ever being invoked, causing unbounded memory retention.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker stores a key with a minimal 2-byte value ('\r\n'), then sends repeated incr/decr commands via the memcached protocol; each command triggers do_item_get (refcount++) followed by the nbytes<=2 early return (no do_item_remove), leaking one refcount per request and causing gradual memory exhaustion.
